title: Quantitative decoupling analysis (QDA v1.0) method
author: Junhua Wang
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The quantitative decoupling analysis (QDA) method presented here,  coupled with the Weather Research and Forecasting-Nested Air Quality Prediction Modeling System (WRF-NAQPMS) model, is an effective tool to quantify the effects of emission, meteorology, chemical reaction, and their nonlinear interactions on the fine particulate matter (PM 2.5 ) pollution, which can be used in both scientific reserach and  air pollution control.
steps_raw: The quantitative decoupling analysis (QDA) method presented here,  coupled with the Weather Research and Forecasting-Nested Air Quality Prediction Modeling System (WRF-NAQPMS) model, is an effective tool to quantify the effects of emission, meteorology, chemical reaction, and their nonlinear interactions on the fine particulate matter (PM 2.5 ) pollution, which can be used in both scientific reserach and  air pollution control.
